content.full_text:
To:        Fair Political Practices Committee
From:      Terry Holbert, Council
           City of Banning
Date:      January 23, 1990


Regarding your letter dated September 11, 1989, file number
I-89-443.

The Councilmember represented in the letter by Best, Best and
Kreiger is me. The response was notably general as admitted in
paragraph titled "foreseeability".

In the interest of trying to more completely understand and
relate to your response I have enclosed the several letters between
BB&K and I on this matter.

I would like a clarification on your use of the word
"processing" in your paragraph called "Conclusion". The use of
this particular word seems to be at odds with Section 18700.1 (b.4
- 5)

Specifically, it would seem to be diametrically opposite to
the statement in item (4) "...except for necessary contact with
agency staff concerning the processing or evaluation of drawing or
submissions prepared by the official.

I would welcome your more direct investigations to insure that
I am not involved in any misconduct, that is certainly not my
intent. I am a Father of 5 and my business is my sole means of
income. While we retain other professionals to examine, review,
and stamp many of our plans, those professionals are not
responsible for day to day contact. Limiting my contact with staff
except as initiated by staff would greatly curtail my ability to
provide needed income for my family.

Business in the City of Banning is about 19% of our overall
business and any abridgement of our normal processing routine would
seem to me to exhaust our profitability and cause extreme distress
to myself, my family and my constituency.

I cannot afford an attorney in this matter, nor do I think it
is warranted, since this entire affair was generated by a statement
of our City Manager which put aside entirely. I am
hopeful that your advice will be a little more specific after you
have reviewed these documents. Naturally I am totally at your
service, my files and my business activities are certainly open to
your perusal on an as needed and voluntary basis.

Thank you for your concern


Terry Holbert

CITY OF BANNING
1434 West Ramsey Street • P.O. Box 998
Banning, California 92220
Telephone (714) 922-1200

OFFICE OF
CITY COUNCIL

City Attorney
City Manager
Fair Campaign Practices Committee
From:
Terry Holbert, Councilman
Date:
January 23, 1990

1.
I would appreciate a letter from the City Attorney outlining
the reasons I requested the attorney to initially write the FPC.
In particular to address the statement, acknowledged by the city
Attorney, myself, and the witness then present which initiated
the contract between Best, Best, and Krieger and the FPPC, which comment
by the City manager was something to this intent.... that I could
perform no Surveying and Engineering within the City of Banning.
Additionally, I would like a letter indicating, whether, in the
opinion of the City Attorney, that issue was decided by the letter,
and if decided whether the City Managers' statement was upheld or
denied.

2.
In as far as possible what other issues were discussed,
including those issues specifically unrelated to any conflict of
interest.
Especially the matter of access to the agenda and the
Road Camp. Even if said references are not terribly substantive.

3.
I am still unclear on several issues, as a matter of fact the
letters seems to have made it more unclear.

Please note the enclosed letter to the FPPC as a reference.

4.
Ray, thank you for your patience and forbearance on this
matter. I have asked you alot of questions, not the least of which
is whether or not there has been a complaint regarding my
activities. I hope I can continue business in Banning under these
trying circumstances. Please notify me of all your concerns, and
those of staff.

Terry Holbert

CITY OF BANNING
1434 West Ramsey Street • P.O. Box 998
Banning, California 92220
Telephone (714) 922-1200

OFFICE OF
CITY COUNCIL

To:    City Attorney
       City Manager
       Fair Campaign Practices Committee
From:  Terry Holbert, Councilman
Date:  January 23, 1990

1.     I would appreciate a letter from the City Attorney outlining
the reasons I requested the attorney to initially write the FPPC
in particular to address the statement, acknowledged by the City
Attorney, myself, and the witness then present which initiated the
contact between Best, Best, and Krieger and the FPPC. Which comment
by the City manager was something to this intent... that I could
perform no Surveying and Engineering within the City of Banning.
Additionally, I would like a letter indicating, whether, in the
opinion of the City Attorney, that issue was decided by the letter,
and if decided whether the City Managers' statement was upheld or
denied.

2.     In as far as possible what other issues were discussed,
including those issues specifically unrelated to any conflict of
interest.  Especially the matter of access to the agenda and the
Road Camp.  Even if said references are not terribly substantive.

3.     I am still unclear on several issues, as a matter of fact the
letters seems to have made it more unclear.

Please note the enclosed letter to the FPPC as a reference.

4.     Ray, thank you for your patience and forbearance on this
matter.  I have asked you a lot of questions, not the least of which
is whether or not there has been a complaint regarding my
activities.  I hope I can continue business in Banning under these
trying circumstances.  Please notify me of all your concerns, and
those of staff.

Terry Holbert

sections.question_synthetic: Terry Holbert, a City of Banning councilmember and surveying/engineering professional, seeks clarification on an FPPC advice letter (R-89-443) regarding the use of the word 'processing' in the Conclusion section and its relationship to Section 18700.1(b)(4), and requests confirmation that the City Manager's statement prohibiting his surveying work in the city was addressed in the FPPC's response.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence from the public to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The document references a prior FPPC advice letter (R-89-443 from September 11, 1989) but does not contain the FPPC's original advice. Multiple copies of similar letters appear in the document. No clear Q&C sections present. Holbert is responding to and questioning aspects of a previous FPPC response.

embedding.summary: This document consists of correspondence from Terry Holbert, a City of Banning councilmember, responding to a previous FPPC advice letter and requesting clarification on the language used regarding 'processing' and its compatibility with conflict of interest statutes. Holbert also requests letters from the City Attorney clarifying the basis for the initial FPPC inquiry and whether the City Manager's prohibition on his surveying work within the city was upheld or denied.
